mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a Rank IC below 0.01, or single-series effects completely dominate the predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52% or Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 changes show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or the signal reverses direction during confirmed expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the inflation signal loses predictive power during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates robust labor demand and construction sector expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or housing data decouples from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in retail sales reflect weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during economic contraction phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during contraction periods, with directional accuracy below 45% or Rank IC below -0.01.
